_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Interface with Senior Management to develop strategic plans for the collection, use and sharing of information in a manner that maximizes its value while complying with applicable privacy regulations.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of privacy-by-design and privacy-by-default principles, including their application to system architecture, data minimization, purpose limitation, storage limitation, and consent management design patterns.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-K-010 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-T-008 (SP, cosine: 0.544), RC-T-013 (RC, cosine: 0.525).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.1/10 (raw 0.411). Shared vocabulary: [management, privacy]. DCWF-only terms [applicable, collection, complying, develop, information, interface] vs WIDAI-only terms [application, architecture, consent, data, default, design] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.855 (P=0.856, R=0.854). Balanced P/R — the 29-word DCWF element and 23-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.7%). "Interface with Senior Management to develop strategic plans for the collection," and "Knowledge of privacy-by-design and privacy-by-default principles, including thei" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.547 > STS 0.411 (gap +0.137). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [management, privacy] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 11.1%. Shared tokens: [management, privacy]. DCWF-only: [applicable, collection, complying, develop, information, interface]. WIDAI-only: [application, architecture, consent, data, default, design].
